chunks.text: New Engineering Center to be named after UC Berkeley alumni Michael and Janelle Grimes. UC Berkeley’s new Engineering Center has been named after alumni Mark and Stephanie Grimes, honoring their contributions to the College of Engineering. The building’s name honors Michael (B.S.’87 EECS) and Janelle Grimes (B.A.’86 PoliSci), who met as undergraduates at UC Berkeley. Michael Grimes would go on to become a successful…
chunks.question: Who is the new Engineering Center named after?

chunks.text: Natacha Crooks named Sloan Fellow. EECS Assistant Professor Natacha Crooks has been awarded a prestigious Sloan Research Fellowship, granted annually to honor exceptional researchers at U.S. and Canadian educational institutions whose creativity, innovation and research accomplishments make them stand out as the next generation of leaders.
chunks.question: What award did Natacha Crooks receive?

chunks.text: Stuart Russell elected to the National Academy of Engineering. EECS Professor Stuart Russell has been elected to the National Academy of Engineering (NAE). Russell was recognized for contributions to developments in artificial intelligence, including reasoning, probabilistic modeling, planning, safety, and education.
chunks.question: Why was Stuart Russell elected to the NAE?

chunks.text: 20 UC Berkeley alumni, students and faculty shine on Forbes 30 Under 30 list. EECS alumni named in Forbes 30 Under 30 list.
chunks.question: What list includes EECS alumni?

chunks.text: Michael I. Jordan named Frontiers of Knowledge Laureate. EECS and Statistics Professor Emeritus and Professor of the Graduate School Michael I. Jordan has been named a Frontiers of Knowledge Laureate. The award recognizes excellence in research and cultural creation, especially contributions with broad impact due to originality and significance.
chunks.question: What honor did Michael I. Jordan receive?

chunks.text: Boubacar Kante named Optica Fellow. EECS Professor Boubacar Kante has been elected as an Optica Fellow, one of the highest honors in the field of optics and photonics. Kante was recognized for creating and demonstrating new concepts in modern electromagnetics, particularly a path toward very-high-power, broad-area single-mode semiconductor lasers.
chunks.question: Why was Boubacar Kante elected as an Optica Fellow?

chunks.text: NBC News: Ion Stoica on DeepSeek. EECS Professor Ion Stoica discusses DeepSeek with Gadi Schwartz on NBC News NOW.
chunks.question: Who discussed DeepSeek on NBC News?

chunks.text: Jennifer Chayes reappointed as CDSS dean for five-year term.
chunks.question: What is Jennifer Chayes' reappointment?

chunks.text: Alexandre Bayen receives honorary doctorate from TU Delft. EECS Professor Alexandre Bayen has been awarded an honorary doctorate by TU Delft. The honorary doctorate was conferred during the 183rd Dies Natalis celebrations, which this year focused on the theme Making Sense of Mobility. Alexandre Bayen studies the collaborative autonomy of manually and automatically driven vehicles.
chunks.question: What did Alexandre Bayen receive from TU Delft?

chunks.text: Berkeley Engineering welcomes eight new faculty members.
chunks.question: How many new faculty members joined Berkeley Engineering?
